title: Incoming Clearing Items
body_text:
Incoming Clearing Items
1	Introduction to Incoming Clearing Items
1.1	Overview of Incoming Clearing Items
This Part describes the processing, settlement, and reporting of Canadian and US dollar Incoming Clearing Items. Procedures on Qualified and Unqualified Items are provided.
Incoming Clearing Items are drawn directly on the credit union or on a member of the credit union. Once the item is cashed or deposited, it is sent through the Clearing to Central 1 and debited to the credit union. On-us Items, items drawn on and deposited to accounts held at the credit union, are received with other Incoming Clearing Items if the credit union sends them on deposit to Central 1.
Qualified Canadian dollar items are classified as In-Province or Out-of-Province Items depending on the regional settlement point through which the items are processed. Qualified US dollar items are processed, settled, and reported separately from Canadian dollar items. The In-Province and Out-of-Province designation applies only to Canadian dollar items.
Changes to the
Bills of Exchange Act
and CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents, extend the same legal standing to cheque images as to physical cleared cheques. As such, it is unnecessary to return physical cleared cheques, including lawyersâ and realtorsâ trust cheques, to credit unions or the credit unionâs members. The Online Return System (ORS) allows authorized users to view, validate, return, and reinstate Incoming Clearing Items.

1.2	Member Statements
Image Files contain images of cleared cheques drawn on membersâ accounts. Central 1 provides Image Files to credit unions or their service providers so that cheque images can be included in membersâ statements. See the Cheque Imaging Part for information on the Image File for member statements.
